evals.prompt: I just installed OpenClaw using Docker and the setup script finished. Now I need to hook it up to Anthropic and OpenAI. Where do I put my API keys and how do I make sure they don't end up hardcoded in the config file?
evals.expected_output: Clear instructions on using ~/.openclaw/.env for API keys with env var interpolation syntax in openclaw.json, covering both Anthropic and OpenAI providers.

assertions.description: Recommends ~/.openclaw/.env as the location for API keys
assertions.expected: Response mentions ~/.openclaw/.env (or ~/.openclaw/.env file) as where to store API keys

assertions.description: Shows ${VAR_NAME} interpolation syntax for use in openclaw.json
assertions.expected: Response includes the ${...} env var interpolation syntax for referencing secrets inside openclaw.json

assertions.description: Covers both Anthropic and OpenAI provider configuration
assertions.expected: Response covers configuration for both anthropic and openai providers

assertions.description: Warns against or avoids recommending hardcoding keys directly in openclaw.json
assertions.expected: Response does not instruct the user to put raw API key strings directly in openclaw.json, either by warning against it or by only showing the env var pattern

evals.prompt: My OpenClaw docker container is running on a home server and I want to access the dashboard from my laptop on the same WiFi network. Right now I can only hit it from the server itself. What do I need to change?
evals.expected_output: Instructions to change gateway.bind from 'loopback' to 'lan', using both the CLI config set command and direct openclaw.json edit, plus security warnings.

assertions.description: Identifies gateway.bind as the setting to change, and specifies 'lan' as the value
assertions.expected: Response clearly identifies changing gateway.bind to 'lan' as the solution

assertions.description: Includes the correct CLI command to change the bind setting
assertions.expected: Response includes 'docker compose run --rm openclaw-cli config set gateway.bind lan' or equivalent

assertions.description: Includes a security warning about not exposing port 18789 to the public internet
assertions.expected: Response warns against exposing the gateway to the public internet or advises caution about network exposure

evals.prompt: I want to add my Telegram bot to OpenClaw so I can chat with Claude through it. I have my bot token from BotFather. Walk me through connecting it.
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step guide to adding Telegram channel via the openclaw-cli channels add command, with correct flags.

assertions.description: Includes the correct 'docker compose run --rm openclaw-cli channels add' command
assertions.expected: Response includes the docker compose run --rm openclaw-cli channels add command

assertions.description: Specifies --channel telegram in the channels add command
assertions.expected: Response includes --channel telegram flag in the channels add command

assertions.description: Specifies --token flag for passing the bot token
assertions.expected: Response includes --token flag (or equivalent) for passing the BotFather token
